regime.label: Cautious Bull
regime.description: Equities recovering from April tariff shock. S&P 500 bouncing off lows but breadth remains narrow. Macro data mixed — inflation declining but growth concerns rising. Defense and energy sectors outperforming. Tech bifurcated on AI spend vs margin compression.

riskMeter.label: Mildly Risk-Off
riskMeter.sublabel: Tariff uncertainty + geopolitical stress keeping allocators defensive

signals.label: S&P 500
signals.note: Recovering from ~5,100 April lows; resistance at 5,700

signals.label: TSX Composite
signals.note: Energy + financials leading; outperforming S&P YTD

signals.label: VIX
signals.note: Off April spike (~35); still elevated vs 2024 baseline

signals.label: Nasdaq / Tech
signals.note: AI names holding; hardware/semis pressured by trade war

signals.label: Fed Funds Rate
signals.note: Fed on hold — inflation sticky near 3%; watching tariff impact

signals.label: 10Y US Treasury
signals.note: Falling on growth fears; flight to safety bid supporting bonds

signals.label: Yield Curve (2s10s)
signals.note: Historical recession precursor — watch for steepening

signals.label: BoC Rate
signals.note: Cutting faster than Fed; further cuts expected H2 2026

signals.label: US CPI (YoY)
signals.note: Declining but last mile sticky; tariffs add re-inflation risk

signals.label: US Unemployment
signals.note: Gradual softening; tech + manufacturing layoffs filtering through

signals.label: Oil (WTI)
signals.note: Off $90+ highs; OPEC+ production + global demand concerns

signals.label: Gold
signals.note: Near all-time highs; safe-haven demand from geopolitical risk

signals.label: USD/CAD
signals.note: BoC cutting faster than Fed offsets commodity support for CAD

signals.label: Fear & Greed
signals.note: Recovering from Extreme Fear (18) in April; not yet neutral

signals.label: AAII Bull/Bear
signals.note: Retail sentiment still negative; often a contrarian buy signal

signals.label: Put/Call Ratio
signals.note: Elevated hedging demand; above 1.0 = peak fear

signals.label: Earnings Revisions
signals.note: Analysts cutting FY2026 EPS estimates on tariff margin impact

scenarios.label: Bull case
scenarios.trigger: US-China trade truce; Fed signals July cut; Q2 earnings beat low bar
scenarios.canadianAngle: TSX 25,500+; oil rebounds on demand recovery; CAD strengthens
scenarios.positioning: Add quality growth (NVDA, AMZN, SHOP); add TFSA tech on dips; reduce cash drag

scenarios.label: Base case
scenarios.trigger: Tariff uncertainty persists; soft landing holds; 1 Fed cut by Sept
scenarios.canadianAngle: TSX sideways 24,000–25,000; energy mixed; CAD range-bound ~1.37–1.40
scenarios.positioning: Hold diversified portfolio; DCA weekly into weakness; keep 10–15% cash buffer

scenarios.label: Bear case
scenarios.trigger: Tariff escalation deepens recession fear; credit stress in CRE; Fed holds
scenarios.canadianAngle: TSX 22,000–23,500; oil back to $55; housing corrections in major cities
scenarios.positioning: Lean into defensives (BRK.B, ENB, RTX); increase ZAG.TO and TLT; hold GOLD

keyEvents.event: FOMC meeting — rate decision + dot plot update
Q2 earnings season — major tech and energy reports

scenarios.label: Bull case
scenarios.trigger: 2–3 Fed cuts; AI capex super-cycle intact; trade war de-escalates
scenarios.canadianAngle: TSX 26,000+; oil $70+; strong Canadian bank earnings; SHOP recovery
scenarios.positioning: Increase equity allocation; rotate into small/mid cap; add INDA, MELI for emerging exposure

scenarios.label: Base case
scenarios.trigger: 1–2 Fed cuts; growth moderates to 1.5–2%; corporate margins hold
scenarios.canadianAngle: TSX 24,000–25,500; CAD stable; REITs recover as BoC cuts accelerate
scenarios.positioning: Stay the course; reinvest dividends; optimize TFSA/RRSP tax placement; add XRE.TO

scenarios.label: Bear case
scenarios.trigger: Recession materializes; credit spreads widen to 500bps; earnings decline 15%+
scenarios.canadianAngle: TSX 20,000–22,000; housing correction accelerates; BoC emergency cuts
scenarios.positioning: Max defensives; increase TLT + ZAG.TO to 20%; hold physical gold via GOLD ETF; reduce leverage

keyEvents.event: FOMC — potential 2nd cut window if data cooperates
Q3 earnings — critical AI infrastructure readthrough
US midterm elections — fiscal policy uncertainty

bullish.label: Fed rate cuts reducing equity discount rates and boosting bond/REIT valuations
AI capex super-cycle driving NVDA, MSFT, AMZN, GOOG earnings above consensus
NATO 2% mandate + Middle East conflict locking in multi-year defense orders (RTX, LMT, NOC)
Gold and CAD energy as structural safe-haven plays independent of rate cycle
GLP-1 drugs expanding healthcare TAM — NVO, ISRG, LLY benefiting from secular demand

bearish.label: US-China tariffs at 145%/125% squeezing corporate margins, particularly tech hardware and retail
Yield curve inversion persisting — historically predicts recession 12–18 months forward
Commercial real estate stress — office vacancy rates at 20%+ with regional bank loan book exposure
US consumer credit card delinquencies at 10-year highs; spending slowdown emerging
Middle East escalation risk — Strait of Hormuz closure would spike oil and shatter supply chains

portfolioImplication.summary: In this environment, your TFSA/RRSP structure is a significant advantage — defensives, gold, and energy in tax-sheltered accounts outperform in a slow-growth regime. Prioritize DCA over lump-sum deployment. Maintain 10–15% cash optionality for a potential H2 dip-buying window.

actions.action: Hold GOLD and ENB as portfolio shock absorbers through the uncertainty window
DCA into quality tech (NVDA, AMZN) — don't try to time the bottom
Consider adding ZAG.TO or TLT in RRSP if bear case probability rises above 35%
Reduce overweight to any single tariff-exposed name >20% of either account
Review INDA / MELI exposure if US-China trade war accelerates Asia supply chain shift
